context: Tests whether the agent generates a production Go Dockerfile with multi-stage builds (builder + runtime), a pinned non-latest base image tag, and a non-root user. The Go binary is compiled in a builder stage and only the final binary is copied to the runtime stage.

checklist.name: Multi-stage build present
checklist.description: Dockerfile contains at least two FROM instructions (a builder stage and a runtime/final stage)

checklist.name: Build tools excluded from runtime
checklist.description: The final stage does NOT use a golang base image — it uses a minimal image (distroless, alpine, scratch, or similar)

checklist.name: Pinned base image tags
checklist.description: Every FROM instruction uses a specific version tag (e.g., golang:1.22-alpine) — NOT :latest or a tag-less reference

checklist.name: Non-root user
checklist.description: Dockerfile creates a non-root user/group and includes a USER instruction before the final CMD/ENTRYPOINT pointing to that non-root user

checklist.name: Binary copied to runtime stage
checklist.description: The compiled binary is copied from the builder stage into the runtime stage using COPY --from=

checklist.name: Absolute WORKDIR
checklist.description: WORKDIR is set to an absolute path (starts with /) in the Dockerfile

checklist.name: EXPOSE port documented
checklist.description: Dockerfile contains an EXPOSE 8080 (or the appropriate port) instruction

checklist.name: Exec-form CMD or ENTRYPOINT
checklist.description: CMD or ENTRYPOINT uses JSON array syntax (e.g., CMD ["/app/server"]) rather than shell string form

checklist.name: .dockerignore created
checklist.description: A .dockerignore file is present alongside the Dockerfile

checklist.name: .dockerignore excludes sensitive paths
checklist.description: .dockerignore contains entries for at least .git, .env (or .env.*), and either node_modules/ or vendor/ or *.exe
